chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Crl. Misc. Appl. No.330 of 2024 (Syed Irshrat Rehan v. The State and others) __________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge __________________________________________________________ Disposed of matter For orders on MA No.5572/24 30.05.2024 Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Khan Jalbani, Advocate for the applicant Dr. Fiaz Shah, Prosecutor General assisted by Mr. Muhammad Anwar Mahar, DDPP for the State a/w I.O of the case ========= The facts, in brief, necessary for the disposal of the instant application are that; the applicant by making an application u/s 561-A Cr.PC impugned before this Court order dated 15.3.2024 passed by learned IXth-Judicial Magistrate Karachi Central on a report furnished by the police under Section 173 Cr.PC whereby he took cognizance of the offence outcome of subject FIR. It was dismissed by this Court vide order dated 15.3.2024; the same is sought to be reviewed by the applicant by way of the listed application. At the very outset, it is stated by learned counsel for the applicant that a supplementary charge sheet in the subject case has been furnished by the police and it has been rejected by the learned trial Magistrate vide order dated 21.03.2024 with a non-speaking order and without providing a chance of hearing to the applicant, in such a changed situation, he would not press the disposal of the listed application before this Court on its merit, provided learned trial Magistrate is directed to pass a afresh order on supplementary challan after providing a chance of hearing to the applicant and all the concerned, which is not opposed by learned PG for the State.

It was dismissed by this Court vide order dated 15.3.2024; the same is sought to be reviewed by the applicant by way of the listed application. At the very outset, it is stated by learned counsel for the applicant that a supplementary charge sheet in the subject case has been furnished by the police and it has been rejected by the learned trial Magistrate vide order dated 21.03.2024 with a non-speaking order and without providing a chance of hearing to the applicant, in such a changed situation, he would not press the disposal of the listed application before this Court on its merit, provided learned trial Magistrate is directed to pass a afresh order on supplementary challan after providing a chance of hearing to the applicant and all the concerned, which is not opposed by learned PG for the State. Order accordingly. J U D G E Nadir*
